title: Task Parallelization Preparation Plan
goal: Prepare relay-dev's control plane for future task parallelization while keeping runtime behavior sequential with max_parallel_jobs=1.
summary: Refactor the single-job execution path into a lease/execute/commit model, add future-proof multi-job state fields, make task phase cursors explicit, and update monitoring surfaces before enabling real parallel execution.
non_goals: Do not enable official max_parallel_jobs > 1 operation.
Do not make same-workspace parallel execution the default.
Do not complete isolated workspace or git worktree merge-back.
Do not launch visible worker terminals per slot.
Do not fully migrate to pending_approvals.
Do not remove compatibility fields current_phase, current_task_id, or active_job_id.

principles.title: Keep sequential behavior stable
principles.description: Every implementation slice must preserve the current one-job-at-a-time cli.ps1 step behavior.

principles.title: Preserve single writer
principles.description: Only the control plane writes events.jsonl and run-state.json. Workers and providers return results but do not mutate canonical state directly.

principles.title: Make commit the only canonicalization gate
principles.description: Provider execution completion is not phase completion. Validation, promotion, event append, state write, and lease clear happen through commit.

principles.title: Keep compatibility views explicit
principles.description: New fields may become canonical candidates, but existing current_phase, current_task_id, and active_job_id remain compatibility projections during this prep phase.

principles.title: Teach monitoring multi-job state first
principles.description: Show, watch, dashboard, and summaries should be able to render active_jobs and task_lane state before real parallel execution is enabled.

fields.active_jobs: Map of job_id to active job lease metadata. Prep phase keeps cardinality <= 1.
fields.task_lane: Task-lane runtime controls such as mode, max_parallel_jobs, and stop_leasing.
fields.task_states[].phase_cursor: Task-local phase cursor for Phase5 through Phase6.
fields.task_states[].wait_reason: Machine-readable reason a task is waiting or blocked.

implementation_steps.title: Extend RunState schema for parallel-ready state
implementation_steps.goal: Add active_jobs, task_lane, state_revision, and task-local cursor fields without changing sequential behavior.
tasks: Add active_jobs and task_lane to newly initialized run-state.
Add state_revision and increment it on state writes.
Add task_states[].phase_cursor, task_states[].active_job_id, and task_states[].wait_reason.
Treat active_job_id as a compatibility view derived from active_jobs where possible.
acceptance: New run-state includes active_jobs, task_lane, and state_revision.
Existing sequential step behavior is unchanged.
Existing code that reads active_job_id still works.
state_revision increases monotonically after state writes.
verification: Run the regression suite or the relevant run-state initialization tests.
Inspect a newly created run-state fixture for the new fields.

implementation_steps.title: Introduce single-job lease model
implementation_steps.goal: Represent the current single active job as an active_jobs entry with lease metadata.
tasks: Create active_jobs[job_id] at dispatch or lease time.
Populate lease_token, lease_expires_at, last_heartbeat_at, lease_owner, slot_id, and workspace_id.
Update active_jobs[job_id] and task_states[task_id].active_job_id under the same run lock.
Keep cli.ps1 step as a single-process lease-then-execute flow for now.
acceptance: At most one active job is present per sequential step.
A job.leased event is emitted after lease creation.
The lease token is carried into the job result or commit path.
Failed recovery can inspect active_jobs as well as the compatibility active_job_id.
verification: Run a fake-provider step and inspect active job metadata during dispatch.

implementation_steps.title: Separate execute and commit boundaries
implementation_steps.goal: Make provider execution completion distinct from canonical commit.
tasks: Move Invoke-EngineStep internals toward lease, execute, and commit function boundaries.
Reload latest run-state at commit start.
Validate lease fencing before artifact promotion or state transition.
Emit job.committed on success and job.commit_rejected on fencing rejection.
acceptance: A successful execute result cannot update canonical artifacts if commit fencing fails.
Successful commit emits job.committed.
Rejected commit emits job.commit_rejected.
Validation failure and repairer flows keep their existing behavior.
verification: Add or run tests for lease token mismatch and expired lease commit rejection.
Run existing repairer and validation regression coverage.

implementation_steps.title: Make task phase cursor a canonical candidate
implementation_steps.goal: Record Phase5 through Phase6 progress on each task state while preserving current coarse run fields.
tasks: Update task_states[task_id].phase_cursor for task-scoped Phase5 through Phase6 transitions.
Keep current_phase and current_task_id as coarse compatibility views.
Move task-local rollback state updates toward task state.
Ensure completed task state drives next task or Phase7 progression.
acceptance: After Phase4-1 go, the selected task phase_cursor is Phase5.
Phase5 -> Phase5-1 -> Phase5-2 -> Phase6 updates are reflected in task state.
Phase6 go marks the task completed and proceeds to the next task or Phase7 as before.
Existing dashboard and status projections remain equivalent for sequential runs.
verification: Run scenario regression for Phase4-1 go through Phase6 go.
Inspect run-state task_states after each task-scoped phase transition.

implementation_steps.title: Make commit transaction explicit
implementation_steps.goal: Group validation, artifact promotion, transition calculation, event append, state write, and lease clear into an explicit commit transaction.
tasks: Define a commit transaction boundary in the control plane.
Keep artifact validation and promotion inside the commit path.
Ensure active job and lease clear occur consistently with event and state updates.
Keep stale or rejected job artifacts as forensic artifacts instead of promoting them.
acceptance: Canonical artifacts are not partially updated when commit fails.
active_jobs is cleared after successful commit.
Expired lease commits are rejected.
Stale job artifacts can remain available for forensic inspection.
verification: Run commit failure regression using fake-provider or fixture artifacts.
Inspect canonical artifact directories after rejected commit.

implementation_steps.title: Extract task-lane summary for monitor and show
implementation_steps.goal: Centralize active job and task lane summary rendering inputs before actual parallel execution exists.
tasks: Add a summary helper that reads run-state and events.
Update cli.ps1 show to display active jobs and task lane counts.
Update watch-run, dashboard-renderer, and run-summary-renderer to consume the same summary where practical.
Add display slots for stale lease, approval wait, dependency wait, and blocked tasks.
acceptance: show displays active jobs and task lane counts.
watch-run no longer depends only on a single current job view.
Stale lease, approval wait, and dependency wait have explicit display fields.
Renderers do not duplicate task-lane inference logic.
verification: Run cli.ps1 show against a fixture state with active_jobs.
Run or inspect watch-run rendering against a fixture state.

implementation_steps.title: Add approval block compatibility behavior
implementation_steps.goal: Keep pending_approval single for now while representing lane-level stop_leasing during approval waits.
tasks: Set task_lane.stop_leasing=true when approval is requested.
Clear stop_leasing when approval is resolved.
Set wait_reason=approval for the relevant task when applicable.
Reserve a future migration path toward pending_approvals without fully implementing it.
acceptance: New leases stop while approval is pending.
stop_leasing is cleared after approval resolution.
The approval target task and phase are visible in monitor or show output.
Existing terminal approval flow still works.
verification: Run approval-request and approval-resolve regression scenarios.
Inspect run-state task_lane and wait_reason during approval wait.

implementation_steps.title: Expand regression coverage for parallelization prep
implementation_steps.goal: Lock in the single-job multi-state behavior so later parallelization cannot regress it.
tasks: Add tests for initial active_jobs and task_lane fields.
Add tests preventing duplicate lease of the same task.
Add tests for lease token mismatch commit rejection.
Add tests for expired lease commit rejection.
Add tests that active_jobs is empty after successful commit.
Add tests for task phase_cursor through Phase5-6 transitions.
Add tests for approval stop_leasing.
Add tests for show or summary output with active_jobs.
acceptance: Regression suite covers lease, commit, stale recovery, task cursor, approval block, and monitor summary prep behavior.
Tests pass in sequential max_parallel_jobs=1 mode.
verification: Run tests/regression.ps1 or the targeted regression subset.
Record any skipped tests and the reason in the worklog.

P0: Existing cli.ps1 step sequential flow passes.
run-state.json has future-proof active_jobs, task_lane, and state_revision fields.
Stale or expired job commits do not update canonical artifacts.
P1: Phase5 through Phase6 task cursor is reflected in task state.
show and watch-run can display active jobs and task lane summary.
Approval wait can be represented with stop_leasing.
Regression tests cover main lease, commit, and monitor cases.
P2: Invoke-EngineStep internals are separated into lease, execute, and commit responsibilities.
A future worker pool can be inserted at the execute boundary.
Future same-workspace parallel and isolated workspace work have clear extension points.

risks.title: Compatibility fields become duplicate sources of truth
risks.mitigation: Keep compatibility projection logic centralized and document which fields are canonical candidates.

risks.title: Commit transaction becomes too large
risks.mitigation: Keep the transaction boundary explicit but split validation, promotion, transition, and event append into helpers.

risks.title: Approval model generalizes too early
risks.mitigation: Use pending_approval plus stop_leasing during this prep plan; leave pending_approvals to a later design.

risks.title: Team treats prep completion as same-workspace parallel approval
risks.mitigation: State clearly that this plan does not enable production parallel execution.

follow_up_plans.title: Visible worker slot launcher plan
scope: monitor, control, and worker slot terminals

llm_execution_guidance.preferred_first_slice: Implement TPP-01 with tests, then stop for review before touching lease execution behavior.
do_not_do: Do not enable more than one active job.
Do not add same-workspace parallel execution.
Do not rewrite unrelated phase logic.
worktree_safety: Read current git status before implementation.
Do not revert unrelated user changes.
Keep changes scoped to the listed candidate files for the selected task.
llm_execution_guidance.verification_policy: Run targeted regression tests for each slice. If full regression is too expensive, record the skipped scope and reason.
